name: mac_audit_logs
explanation: Mac Audit Logs is a logs-only integration. The check runs locally on a macOS host with BSM auditing enabled and shells out to `auditreduce` / `praudit` (via sudo) to parse files in /var/audit. There is no remote network endpoint to probe — discovery would reduce to 'is BSM auditing enabled and is the agent running on a Mac with sudo access to auditreduce?'. The required `AUDIT_LOGS_DIR_PATH` is conventionally /var/audit but the operating model is host-local log file ingestion, not a service probe; auto-config in the network-probe sense does not apply.
notes: Logs-only integration that subprocesses local BSM tooling (auditreduce/praudit) with sudo. Not a network-probe target. Similar shape to other host-local log forwarders (Pattern N analogue for macOS audit pipeline).
